title: How to Use Intuition When Making Health Decisions
meta_description: Discover how to trust your gut feelings in health decisions. Learn to listen to your body's signals when medical advice falls short.
og_title: How to Use Intuition When Making Health Decisions
og_description: Discover how to trust your gut feelings in health decisions. Learn to listen to your body's signals when medical advice falls short.
body:
You've Been Gaslit by the Medical System (And Your Intuition Is the Antidote)

Let me guess. You've walked into a doctor's office with a gut feeling something was off. You couldn't quite explain it. You just knew. And then you got told you were anxious. Overthinking. Being dramatic. Maybe even "too sensitive."

I've been there. You've been there. And I'm here to tell you: that feeling wasn't the problem. The problem was you didn't trust it.

Here's the thing nobody tells you about health decisions: your body is having a conversation with you all day, every day. It's whispering, sometimes shouting, through sensations, hunches, and that weird knowing you can't logic your way out of. The question isn't whether you have intuition. The question is whether you're brave enough to listen when the evidence doesn't match.

The body doesn't lie. It can't. It doesn't have the vocabulary for manipulation. When your gut says no and the test says yes, the test is incomplete - not you.

Right? So let's get into how you actually use this skill without losing your mind or ignoring real medical help.

Why Your Intuition Gets Drowned Out in a Doctor's Office

Think about the last time you sat in a sterile exam room. Fluorescent lights. Paper gown. A person in a white coat holding a clipboard. The power dynamic alone is enough to shut down your inner voice. You're trained from childhood to defer to authority, especially medical authority. And when that authority tells you "everything looks fine," your gut gets overruled by your conditioning.

But here's what I've learned from working with hundreds of sensitive people: your nervous system picks up on things before your conscious mind does. It reads the doctor's hesitation. It registers the subtle shift in their energy when they skip over a lab result. It feels the incongruence between their words and their tone. That's not anxiety. That's data.

The problem is you've been taught to ignore that data because it doesn't fit the story everyone else is telling. So you leave the appointment feeling confused, dismissed, and somehow convinced you're the one who's broken.

You're not broken. You're just operating on a wider bandwidth than most people. And that's a gift - once you learn how to use it.

The Three Layers of Intuitive Health Decisions

Let me break this down into something you can actually work with. When I talk about using intuition for health, I'm not talking about ditching your doctor and reading tea leaves instead. I'm talking about layering your inner knowing on top of outer information. Here are the three layers you need to check before making any health decision:

Layer One: The Body Signal
This is the physical sensation. The knot in your stomach when a treatment plan is presented. The lightness in your chest when someone suggests a different approach. The fatigue that hits you when you think about following through on a recommendation. Your body is the most honest thing you've got. It doesn't care about pleasing anyone. It just responds.

Layer Two: The Emotional Resonance
How do you feel about the decision? Not what you think you should feel. Not what your partner or parent or doctor wants you to feel. What's the emotional tone underneath? Is there dread? Relief? A quiet sense of "yes" that doesn't need to shout? Your emotions are not your enemy. They're your compass.

Layer Three: The Knowing Without Evidence
This is the weird one. The one that makes you sound crazy if you say it out loud. It's the thought that pops in from nowhere: "This isn't the right specialist" or "That medication won't work for me." You have no proof. You just know. Trust that. It's the most refined form of intuition you have.

Does that land? Good. Now let's get practical.

How to Actually Use Intuition in Real-Time Health Decisions

I'm not going to give you some abstract theory. Here's the step-by-step process I teach my clients. It works whether you're deciding on a surgery, choosing a supplement, or figuring out if you need a second opinion.

Step One: Get Quiet Before You Get Loud

Before you make any health decision, you need to create space. That means no phone. No input from other people. No Google rabbit holes. Just you and your breath. Sit for five minutes and ask yourself one question: "What do I already know about this?" Don't try to answer. Just let the answer surface. It might come as a word, a feeling, or a physical sensation. Write it down.

Step Two: Check the Body First

Bring the decision into your awareness. Imagine you've already chosen Option A. Scan your body from head to toe. Where do you feel tension? Where do you feel ease? Now imagine Option B. Same scan. The body doesn't lie. If one option makes your shoulders drop and the other makes your stomach clench, you have your answer - even if the logic says otherwise.

Step Three: Separate Fear from Intuition

This is the tricky part. Fear and intuition can feel identical in the body. Both can create tightness, racing thoughts, and avoidance. The difference is in the quality. Fear feels contracted, urgent, and desperate. It says "I need to fix this NOW." Intuition feels more like a calm knowing. It doesn't need to convince you. It just sits there, patient, waiting for you to catch up.

If you're not sure which one you're feeling, ask yourself: "If I had all the time in the world, what would I choose?" Fear collapses under that question. Intuition stays steady.

When Intuition Says No and the Experts Say Yes

This is the moment that separates the people who heal from the people who stay stuck. You've got a diagnosis. A treatment plan. A specialist who's published papers and has a wall of degrees. And everything in you is screaming "no."

What do you do?

First, you don't ignore the expert. You're not trying to replace their knowledge. You're trying to integrate it with your own. So you ask questions. You get a second opinion. You research until you understand why your intuition is saying no. Sometimes it's because you're afraid of the side effects. Sometimes it's because your body knows the treatment won't work for your specific biochemistry. Sometimes it's because the doctor is wrong.

I've had clients whose intuition saved them from unnecessary surgeries. I've also had clients whose intuition told them to push for a test their doctor said wasn't needed - and that test found something early. The key is not to pit intuition against medicine. It's to use intuition as a filter for medicine.

If you want a resource that helps you bridge this gap, I recommend When the Body Says No by Gabor Maté. It's not a light read, but it will change how you understand the relationship between stress, intuition, and physical health. See on Amazon (paid link)

Building Your Intuitive Health Muscle (It's a Practice, Not a Gift)

Here's what nobody tells you: intuition is a skill. You can strengthen it just like you'd strengthen a muscle. And the way you strengthen it is by using it in low-stakes situations first.

Start with small health decisions. What does your body want for breakfast today? Not what you "should" eat. What actually sounds good? Notice the difference between a craving (which is often addictive) and a deep knowing (which feels like nourishment).

Move to movement. Does your body want to stretch, walk, or rest? Don't override it with your workout plan. Listen.

Then graduate to supplements, appointments, and treatments. Each time you listen and act on what you hear, you build trust with yourself. Each time you ignore it, you erode that trust. It's that simple.

Journaling Prompts to Sharpen Your Intuition

If you're not sure where to start, try these prompts. Write without editing. Let the pen move even if it feels like nonsense:

  • "If my body could speak about this health issue, it would say..."
  • "The part of me that knows what's best for my health is telling me..."
  • "I keep ignoring the feeling that..."
  • "If I trusted myself completely, I would..."

Don't judge what comes up. Just let it land.

Why Sensitive People Have an Edge Here

You know that thing where you walk into a room and feel the mood before anyone speaks? Or you meet someone and instantly know something's off about them? That's not paranoia. That's your nervous system picking up on subtle cues that most people miss. And when it comes to health, that sensitivity is a superpower.

Sensitive people can feel the quality of a treatment before they try it. They can sense whether a doctor is truly present or just going through the motions. They can feel the difference between a medication that's right for them and one that's not - even before the side effects show up.

The problem is that sensitivity has been pathologized. You've been told you're "too much," "too emotional," "too picky." So you learn to shut it down. But shutting down your sensitivity doesn't make you healthier. It makes you disconnected from the only source of guidance that's actually customized to your body.

If you want to understand your sensitivity as a gift rather than a curse, pick up The Highly Sensitive Person by Elaine Aron. It's the book that helped me understand myself. See on Amazon (paid link)

Red Flags: When Intuition Can Lead You Astray

I'm not going to romanticize this. Intuition can be wrong. Not because it's not real, but because it gets contaminated by fear, wishful thinking, or trauma. Here's how to tell the difference between clean intuition and noise:

  • Clean intuition is calm, consistent, and doesn't need to convince anyone. It feels like a quiet "yes" or a firm "no."
  • Noisy intuition (fear, trauma, conditioning) feels frantic, urgent, and often comes with a story. "I can't do this because something bad will happen."
  • Wishful thinking feels like hope without grounding. "I just know this will work even though I have no evidence."

The way to clean up your intuition is to ground it in reality. Get the test. Get the second opinion. Do the research. Then check back in with your body. Clean intuition will still be there after you've gathered information. Noise will dissolve when exposed to facts.

A Quick Reality Check Process

Before you act on a strong intuitive hit about health, run it through this filter:

  1. Does this align with basic medical knowledge? (Not all of it, but the non-negotiable stuff?)
  2. Have I checked in with at least one trusted professional?
  3. Does this feel like expansion or contraction in my body?
  4. Would I still feel this way if I were alone and nobody was watching?
  5. Can I wait 24 hours before deciding?

If you answer "yes" to most of these, you're likely on solid ground.

The Integration: Using Both Logic and Intuition

I get asked all the time: "Should I trust my gut or the test results?" And the answer is both. You don't have to choose. Intuition and logic are not enemies. They're partners.

Think of it this way: logic gives you the map. Intuition tells you which roads are safe to travel. The map shows you the terrain, but only your body knows if that path is right for you.

So when you're facing a health decision, gather the data. Read the studies. Talk to the experts. Get the scans. And then sit with it all and ask your body: "What do you think?"

Sometimes the answer will surprise you. Sometimes it will confirm what you already knew. Either way, you're making a decision from a place of integration, not fragmentation.

For a guide on how to integrate intuition with decision-making in all areas of life, I recommend The Gift of Fear by Gavin de Becker. It's about safety, but the principles apply to health decisions too. See on Amazon (paid link)

What To Do When You've Ignored Your Intuition (And Now You're Stuck)

Maybe you're reading this and thinking, "Great. I ignored my gut for years. I made decisions based on what everyone else told me. And now I'm dealing with the consequences. What do I do?"

First, forgive yourself. You did the best you could with what you knew. The fact that you're here, reading this, means you're ready to do it differently. That's enough.

Second, start small. You don't have to undo years of disconnection overnight. Pick one health decision this week - even a tiny one - and practice listening. Maybe it's choosing a meal. Maybe it's deciding whether to take a nap or push through. Maybe it's canceling an appointment you knew was wrong for you.

Third, rebuild trust with yourself by keeping your word. If your intuition says "don't eat that," don't eat it. If it says "call that practitioner," call them. Every time you follow through, you send a message to your subconscious: "I'm listening. I'm reliable. I've got your back."

And if you're deep in a health crisis and don't know where to start, get support. Find a practitioner who honors your intuition. Work with a coach or therapist who understands the sensitive nervous system. You don't have to do this alone.

One book that helped me work through a health crisis with both intuition and logic is Radical Hope by Kelly Turner. It's about the mindset of healing. See on Amazon (paid link)

The Bottom Line: You Already Know What to Do

I know this sounds too simple, but it's true. You already know what your body needs. You've always known. The only thing that's been in the way is the noise - the fear, the conditioning, the voices telling you that you're not qualified to know.

But you are qualified. Your body has been with you your whole life. It's been through every illness, every treatment, every sleepless night. It knows what works and what doesn't. The question is whether you're ready to trust it.

Start today. Pick one health decision and check in with your body before you make it. Notice what comes up. Write it down. Act on it if it feels right. And then notice what happens.

You're not crazy. You're not too sensitive. You're just finally listening to the voice that's been trying to help you all along.

Ready to go deeper? Take the free assessment at intuitivesawaken.com/assessment to find out where your intuitive blocks are and how to clear them. It takes five minutes and it might just change everything.
